mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced economic activity, causing short-term equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power during late-cycle expansions, with forward 5-day returns showing a positive mean despite negative INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPI exceeds 3.0% annually, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and wage growth, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or payroll growth shows no correlation with 60-day forward equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales indicates a shift in consumer spending toward durable goods, creating sector rotation that negatively impacts broad S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05), or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in industrial production combined with stable inflation signals strong supply-side momentum, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances during early-cycle recoveries, or the predictive accuracy drops below chance levels.
